title: Identify the OptiSweep System, AGV, and Tote Control Areas on the ACB API Screen
summary: Use the documented ACB API screen layout to identify which screen areas correspond to the OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes before reviewing or performing actions on that screen.
when_to_use: Use this reference when navigating to the ACB API screen and confirming which numbered screen areas correspond to the OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes.
not_for: Do not use this runbook to perform specific system, AGV, or tote control actions, because the source provided here only supports identification of the control domains on the ACB API screen.
Do not use this runbook to interpret alternate or modified ACB API layouts not matching the documented three-domain screen.

responsibilities: Access the ACB API screen from the ACB System screen.
Use the documented numbered layout to identify the OptiSweep system, AGV, and tote control areas.
Confirm the intended action belongs to the correct control domain before proceeding.

safety_notes: This runbook is a screen-identification reference only.
If the displayed screen does not match the documented three-domain layout, stop and escalate because the source provides no alternate layout interpretation.
access_or_tools_needed: Figure 4-8 or equivalent documented screen reference

system_preconditions: The user is able to reach the "ACB System" screen.
The ACB API screen is accessible by pressing API on the "ACB System" screen.

visual_references.description: System HMI screen for the ACB API interface used to control the OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes.
System HMI screen showing the ACB System Overview interface that provides context for the source screen from which API is accessed.
System API Controls figure showing the system control area associated with the ACB API screen.
AGV API Controls figure showing the AGV control area associated with the ACB API screen.
Tote-related API screen artifact associated with the tote control area context.
Tote exchange settings screen artifact associated with tote control context.

steps.title: Open the ACB API screen
steps.purpose: Access the documented screen where the three control domains are shown.
steps.instruction: From the "ACB System" screen, press API to access the ACB API screen.
steps.expected_result: The ACB API screen is displayed.
steps.healthy_condition: The displayed screen matches the documented ACB API screen referenced in Figure 4-8.
steps.failure_condition: The API screen does not open, or the displayed screen does not match the documented ACB API screen.

actions.label: Open API screen
actions.exact_action: Press API on the "ACB System" screen to access this screen.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The ACB API interface used to control the OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes.
The ACB System screen context from which the API button is accessed.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not match the documented ACB API screen layout.
The API screen cannot be accessed from the "ACB System" screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: Press API on the "ACB System" screen to access this screen.

steps.title: Locate the numbered control areas
steps.purpose: Find the three numbered areas on the ACB API screen that define the documented control domains.
steps.instruction: On the ACB API screen, locate the areas labeled as items (1), (2), and (3) using Figure 4-8 as the reference.
steps.expected_result: The three numbered screen areas are identified on the displayed ACB API screen.
steps.healthy_condition: Items (1), (2), and (3) are visible and correspond to the documented numbered layout in Figure 4-8.
steps.failure_condition: The numbered areas are not visible, are unclear, or do not correspond to the documented layout.

actions.label: Find numbered areas
actions.exact_action: Locate the screen areas labeled as items (1), (2), and (3).

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Figure 4-8 showing the labeled screen sections and numbered items (1), (2), and (3).

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not show the documented three numbered control areas.
The screen layout differs from Figure 4-8.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep system (1), AGVs (2), and totes (3) can be controlled through this screen.

steps.title: Identify item (1) as the OptiSweep system control area
steps.purpose: Associate the first numbered area with the correct control domain.
steps.instruction: Identify item (1) on the ACB API screen as the OptiSweep system control area.
steps.expected_result: Item (1) is recognized as the OptiSweep system control area.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can correctly associate item (1) with the OptiSweep system.
steps.failure_condition: Item (1) is misidentified or cannot be matched to the OptiSweep system.

actions.label: Identify system area
actions.exact_action: Identify item (1) as the OptiSweep system control area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The numbered item (1) on the ACB API screen.
The system control area context associated with the System section of the API screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Item (1) cannot be confidently matched to the OptiSweep system using the documented screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep system (1), AGVs (2), and totes (3) can be controlled through this screen.

steps.title: Identify item (2) as the AGV control area
steps.purpose: Associate the second numbered area with the correct control domain.
steps.instruction: Identify item (2) on the ACB API screen as the AGV control area.
steps.expected_result: Item (2) is recognized as the AGV control area.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can correctly associate item (2) with AGVs.
steps.failure_condition: Item (2) is misidentified or cannot be matched to AGVs.

actions.label: Identify AGV area
actions.exact_action: Identify item (2) as the AGV control area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The numbered item (2) on the ACB API screen.
The AGV control area context associated with the AGV section of the API screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: Item (2) cannot be confidently matched to AGVs using the documented screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep system (1), AGVs (2), and totes (3) can be controlled through this screen.

steps.title: Identify item (3) as the tote control area
steps.purpose: Associate the third numbered area with the correct control domain.
steps.instruction: Identify item (3) on the ACB API screen as the tote control area.
steps.expected_result: Item (3) is recognized as the tote control area.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can correctly associate item (3) with totes.
steps.failure_condition: Item (3) is misidentified or cannot be matched to totes.

actions.label: Identify tote area
actions.exact_action: Identify item (3) as the tote control area.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The numbered item (3) on the ACB API screen.
Tote-related API context associated with the tote section of the API screen.
Additional tote control context related to tote exchange settings.

stop_or_escalate_if: Item (3) cannot be confidently matched to totes using the documented screen.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep system (1), AGVs (2), and totes (3) can be controlled through this screen.

steps.title: Confirm the intended action matches the correct control domain
steps.purpose: Prevent misinterpretation of the screen by ensuring the intended review or action is associated with the correct domain.
steps.instruction: Before proceeding with any review or control action, verify that the action you intend to review or perform is associated with the correct domain: OptiSweep system, AGVs, or totes.
steps.expected_result: The intended action is matched to the correct control domain on the ACB API screen.
steps.healthy_condition: The user can clearly determine whether the intended action belongs to the system, AGV, or tote area.
steps.failure_condition: The intended action cannot be confidently associated with one of the three documented domains.

actions.label: Verify domain before proceeding
actions.exact_action: Verify that the control action you intend to review or perform is associated with the correct domain before proceeding.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: The three documented control domains on the ACB API screen.

stop_or_escalate_if: The displayed screen does not match the documented three-domain layout.
The intended action cannot be associated with the correct domain using the source-provided screen reference.

source_evidence.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep system (1), AGVs (2), and totes (3) can be controlled through this screen.

success_criteria: The ACB API screen is accessed from the "ACB System" screen.
The user identifies the three documented control domains on the ACB API screen.
Item (1) is identified as the OptiSweep system control area.
Item (2) is identified as the AGV control area.
Item (3) is identified as the tote control area.
The intended review or action is confirmed against the correct domain before proceeding.
healthy_conditions: The three numbered areas are visible and interpretable.
The user can distinguish the three documented control domains on the ACB API screen: OptiSweep system, AGVs, and totes.
failure_conditions: The displayed screen does not match the documented three-domain layout.
The numbered areas cannot be located or interpreted from the displayed screen.
The user cannot confidently determine whether an intended action belongs to the system, AGV, or tote domain.
escalation_guidance: Escalate if the displayed screen does not match the documented three-domain layout, because the source provides no alternate layout interpretation.
Escalate if the ACB API screen cannot be accessed from the "ACB System" screen using the documented API navigation.
Escalate if the intended action cannot be associated with one of the three documented control domains using the source-provided reference.

screens_or_images.what_to_look_at: Primary reference image for the ACB API screen and its numbered control domains.
Context for the ACB System screen from which API is accessed.

source_refs.quote_or_summary: The OptiSweep system (1), AGVs (2), and totes (3) can be controlled through this screen.
Press API on the "ACB System" screen to access this screen.

missing_details: The source does not provide an estimated completion time.
The source does not define specific controls or actions available within each domain in this section.
The source does not specify alternate layouts, troubleshooting steps, or recovery actions if the screen differs from Figure 4-8.
The source does not specify production stop or LOTO requirements for this reference activity.
